blurb: Supports Alberta's workforce and economic growth through employment standards enforcement, workplace health and safety, labour relations, trade and investment attraction, provincial immigration pathways, newcomer settlement and integration services, and multiculturalism and inclusion initiatives.

goals.title: OUTCOME 1: Alberta workplaces are safe, fair, and meet employment standards
goals.description: The ministry sets and enforces employment standards for wages, hours, leaves, and termination; administers occupational health and safety requirements; and oversees labour relations including collective bargaining, mediation, and essential services agreements.

foundational_services.name: Develop employment, labour, and workplace safety policy
foundational_services.desc: Develops policy and legislation governing employment standards, occupational health and safety, and labour relations in Alberta. Monitors workplace trends, consults with employer and worker stakeholders, and updates regulatory frameworks to reflect modern work arrangements.
foundational_services.users: Government policy staff, employer associations, labour organizations

relationships.service: File a complaint about unpaid wages, wrongful termination, or other employment standards violations
Report a workplace health or safety concern or incident

main_services.name: Understand or enforce your employment rights

supporting.name: Learn about minimum wage, hours of work, overtime, and leave entitlements
supporting.desc: Workers and employers can access information on Alberta's employment standards including minimum wage rates, hours of work and rest periods, overtime rules, vacation and holiday pay, and leave entitlements such as maternity, parental, compassionate care, and personal leaves.
supporting.users: Workers, employers, payroll administrators, HR professionals

reach_estimate.estimate: Millions of workers and hundreds of thousands of employers province-wide
reach_estimate.basis: Employment standards apply to virtually every workplace in Alberta

economic_impact.note: Clear employment standards protect workers and provide certainty for employers

relationships.service: File a complaint about unpaid wages, wrongful termination, or other employment standards violations
Earn credits through work experience or RAP

supporting.name: File a complaint about unpaid wages, wrongful termination, or other employment standards violations
supporting.desc: Workers who believe their employer has violated employment standards — such as withholding wages, failing to pay overtime, or wrongful termination — can file a complaint with Employment Standards for investigation. Officers can order employers to pay owed wages and penalties.
supporting.users: Workers experiencing employment standards violations

reach_estimate.estimate: Thousands of complaints filed annually
reach_estimate.basis: Employment Standards processes thousands of complaints each year resulting in millions in recovered wages

economic_impact.note: Complaint enforcement deters wage theft and protects vulnerable workers

relationships.service: Learn about minimum wage, hours of work, overtime, and leave entitlements
Learn about rules for termination, layoff, and severance
Develop employment, labour, and workplace safety policy

supporting.name: Learn about rules for termination, layoff, and severance
supporting.desc: Workers and employers can access information on termination notice requirements, temporary layoff rules, group termination obligations, and severance entitlements under Alberta's employment standards. Includes tools for calculating minimum notice and termination pay.
supporting.users: Workers facing termination, employers managing layoffs, HR professionals

reach_estimate.estimate: Hundreds of thousands of page views annually
reach_estimate.basis: Termination rules are among the most-searched employment standards topics

economic_impact.note: Clear termination rules protect workers from unfair dismissal and help employers manage workforce changes lawfully

relationships.service: File a complaint about unpaid wages, wrongful termination, or other employment standards violations

main_services.name: Ensure your workplace meets health and safety requirements

supporting.name: Learn about workplace health and safety obligations and rights
supporting.desc: Workers and employers can access information on OHS obligations including hazard assessments, safety training, right to refuse unsafe work, joint work site health and safety committees, and industry-specific safety codes for construction, oil and gas, and other sectors.
supporting.users: Workers, employers, safety officers, joint health and safety committee members

reach_estimate.estimate: All Alberta workplaces — over 200,000 employers
reach_estimate.basis: OHS legislation applies to virtually every employer in the province

economic_impact.note: Workplace safety reduces injuries, fatalities, and the economic burden of workplace incidents

relationships.service: Report a workplace health or safety concern or incident

supporting.name: Report a workplace health or safety concern or incident
supporting.desc: Workers, employers, and the public can report serious workplace injuries, fatalities, or unsafe work conditions to OHS. Inspectors investigate complaints, conduct inspections, and can issue stop-work orders, compliance orders, and administrative penalties.
supporting.users: Workers in unsafe conditions, employers reporting incidents, concerned members of the public

reach_estimate.estimate: Thousands of complaints and incident reports annually
reach_estimate.basis: OHS conducts thousands of inspections and investigations each year

economic_impact.note: Incident reporting and enforcement prevents future injuries and saves lives

relationships.service: Learn about workplace health and safety obligations and rights
Develop employment, labour, and workplace safety policy

main_services.name: Resolve a labour relations dispute or access collective bargaining supports

supporting.name: Apply for mediation or arbitration of a collective bargaining dispute
supporting.desc: Employers and unions involved in a collective bargaining dispute can apply for government mediation services to help reach a settlement. Where mediation is unsuccessful, arbitration may be available. The ministry also administers essential services agreements to maintain critical public services during labour disputes.
supporting.users: Unions, employers in collective bargaining, essential service employers

reach_estimate.estimate: Hundreds of mediation and arbitration cases annually
reach_estimate.basis: Alberta has hundreds of active collective agreements with periodic disputes requiring intervention

economic_impact.note: Effective dispute resolution prevents work stoppages that disrupt the economy and essential services

supporting.name: File an unfair labour practice complaint
supporting.desc: Workers, unions, or employers who believe an unfair labour practice has occurred — such as interference with union organizing, bad-faith bargaining, or intimidation — can file a complaint with the Alberta Labour Relations Board for investigation and adjudication.
supporting.users: Workers, union representatives, employers

reach_estimate.estimate: Hundreds of ULP complaints annually
reach_estimate.basis: The Labour Relations Board adjudicates unfair labour practice complaints from across Alberta

economic_impact.note: ULP enforcement protects workers' organizing rights and ensures fair collective bargaining

goals.title: OUTCOME 2: Alberta's economy is diversified and globally competitive through trade and investment
goals.description: The ministry attracts investment, supports businesses in accessing global markets, administers economic development programs including the film and television tax credit, and provides economic data and analysis to support business and policy decisions.

foundational_services.name: Develop trade, investment, and economic development strategy
foundational_services.desc: Develops Alberta's trade and investment strategy, maintains international trade offices, negotiates trade agreements, manages investor relationships, and administers economic development programs including sector-specific incentives to diversify and grow Alberta's economy.
foundational_services.users: Government policy staff, international trade offices, economic development agencies

relationships.service: Access Alberta economic data, statistics, and labour market information

main_services.name: Access business supports and investment services

supporting.name: Get help expanding your business or accessing new markets
supporting.desc: Alberta businesses can access trade missions, export readiness programs, market intelligence, and connection services to help them grow and sell into new domestic and international markets. The ministry operates international trade offices and partners with federal trade commissioners.
supporting.users: Alberta businesses seeking to export or expand, international investors

reach_estimate.estimate: Thousands of businesses access trade supports annually
reach_estimate.basis: Alberta exports over $150 billion in goods annually with growing diversification

economic_impact.note: Trade support directly enables market access and revenue growth for Alberta businesses

supporting.name: Invest in Alberta or relocate your business to the province
supporting.desc: International and domestic companies considering Alberta as a location for investment, expansion, or relocation can access information on the province's competitive advantages, available incentives, sector strengths, and connection services through Invest Alberta and the ministry.
supporting.users: International companies, domestic firms considering relocation, investors

reach_estimate.estimate: Hundreds of investment inquiries and projects annually
reach_estimate.basis: Invest Alberta actively manages a pipeline of investment attraction opportunities

economic_impact.note: Investment attraction creates jobs, diversifies the economy, and generates tax revenue

main_services.name: Access economic data and sector incentive programs

supporting.name: Apply for the Alberta film and television tax credit
supporting.desc: Film and television productions shooting in Alberta can apply for the provincial film and television tax credit. The program provides a refundable tax credit to eligible productions, supporting the growth of Alberta's creative industries and attracting productions to the province.
supporting.users: Film and television producers, production companies, studios

channels.label: Film and television tax credit application

reach_estimate.estimate: Dozens of productions apply annually
reach_estimate.basis: Growing film industry with increasing production activity in Alberta

economic_impact.note: Film tax credits attract productions that generate local spending, jobs, and industry capacity

supporting.name: Access Alberta economic data, statistics, and labour market information
supporting.desc: Businesses, researchers, and the public can access Alberta's economic data including GDP, employment statistics, industry profiles, labour market forecasts, and regional economic indicators to support business planning and policy decisions.
supporting.users: Businesses, economists, researchers, students, government planners

reach_estimate.estimate: Hundreds of thousands of data views and report downloads annually
reach_estimate.basis: Widely used by business, academia, and government for economic planning

economic_impact.note: Reliable economic data supports informed business investment and policy decisions

relationships.service: Develop trade, investment, and economic development strategy

goals.title: OUTCOME 3: Newcomers can immigrate to Alberta through provincial pathways
goals.description: The ministry operates the Alberta Advantage Immigration Program, which nominates skilled workers and entrepreneurs for permanent residence, and provides information on immigration streams, eligibility, and application processes.

foundational_services.name: Design and manage provincial immigration policy and nomination streams
foundational_services.desc: Develops immigration stream criteria, manages nomination allocations with the federal government, monitors labour market needs, and adjusts program design to attract workers and entrepreneurs who meet Alberta's economic priorities.
foundational_services.users: Government policy staff, federal immigration partners

main_services.name: Apply for provincial nomination as a skilled worker

supporting.name: Apply through the Alberta Opportunity Stream
supporting.desc: Workers currently employed in Alberta in an eligible occupation can apply for a provincial nomination for permanent residence through the Alberta Opportunity Stream. Applicants must meet criteria for work experience, language proficiency, and education.
supporting.users: Temporary foreign workers and residents working in Alberta

reach_estimate.estimate: Thousands of nominations annually
reach_estimate.basis: Major worker stream with significant allocation within Alberta's total nomination quota

economic_impact.note: Addresses labour shortages by providing permanent residence pathways for workers already contributing to Alberta's economy

supporting.name: Apply through the Alberta Express Entry Stream
supporting.desc: Candidates in the federal Express Entry pool with strong ties to Alberta or skills in priority sectors (health care, technology, policing) can receive a provincial notification of interest and apply for an Alberta nomination, boosting their federal ranking.
supporting.users: Federal Express Entry candidates with Alberta connections or priority skills

reach_estimate.estimate: Thousands of nominations annually
reach_estimate.basis: High-demand stream targeting skilled workers in priority sectors

economic_impact.note: Attracts highly skilled professionals in health care, technology, and other priority sectors

supporting.name: Apply through the Rural Renewal Stream or Tourism and Hospitality Stream
supporting.desc: Workers in rural Alberta communities or in tourism and hospitality roles can apply for nomination through dedicated streams designed to address labour needs in smaller communities and the service sector.
supporting.users: Workers in rural Alberta or tourism/hospitality sector

reach_estimate.estimate: Hundreds of nominations annually per stream
reach_estimate.basis: Targeted streams for specific sectors and communities

economic_impact.note: Sustains rural communities and tourism sector by addressing chronic labour shortages

main_services.name: Apply for provincial nomination as an entrepreneur

supporting.name: Apply to start or buy a business in Alberta as an entrepreneur
supporting.desc: International entrepreneurs can apply for provincial nomination through the Rural Entrepreneur Stream, Graduate Entrepreneur Stream, Farm Stream, or Foreign Graduate Entrepreneur Stream by proposing a viable business plan and investing in Alberta's economy.
supporting.users: International entrepreneurs and investors seeking permanent residence

reach_estimate.estimate: Hundreds of entrepreneur applications annually
reach_estimate.basis: Multiple entrepreneur streams attracting business investment

economic_impact.note: Entrepreneur immigration brings capital investment, job creation, and business diversification

supporting.name: Check your immigration application status
supporting.desc: Applicants to any Alberta Advantage Immigration Program stream can check the status of their application through the online portal. Processing times and application requirements vary by stream.
supporting.users: AAIP applicants awaiting decisions

reach_estimate.estimate: Tens of thousands of status checks annually
reach_estimate.basis: All AAIP applicants check status multiple times during processing

economic_impact.note: Transparent status tracking reduces applicant anxiety and inquiry volume

goals.title: OUTCOME 4: Newcomers settle, integrate, and thrive in a welcoming and inclusive Alberta
goals.description: The ministry supports newcomers in settling and integrating into Alberta communities through settlement services, language training, foreign credential recognition, and employment supports, while promoting multiculturalism, combating racism, and advancing human rights.

foundational_services.name: Fund and coordinate settlement, integration, and multiculturalism programs
foundational_services.desc: Administers settlement and integration funding for community organizations, coordinates multiculturalism grant programs, supports the Anti-Racism Advisory Council and the Premier's Council on Multiculturalism, and develops policy on inclusion, diversity, and human rights.
foundational_services.users: Settlement service agencies, cultural organizations, advisory councils

relationships.service: File a human rights complaint about discrimination

main_services.name: Get help settling in Alberta as a newcomer

supporting.name: Find settlement services and newcomer supports in your community
supporting.desc: Newcomers can find funded settlement organizations in their community that provide orientation, language assessment, employment assistance, and community connections to help them establish their new life in Alberta.
supporting.users: New immigrants, refugees, temporary residents transitioning to permanent residence

reach_estimate.estimate: Tens of thousands of newcomers served annually
reach_estimate.basis: Alberta receives over 50,000 new permanent residents per year

economic_impact.note: Effective settlement accelerates economic participation and reduces social costs

supporting.name: Access language training and assessment
supporting.desc: Newcomers can access English language training, language assessment services, and bridging programs funded through provincial settlement programs. Language proficiency is key to employment and community integration.
supporting.users: Newcomers needing English language support

reach_estimate.estimate: Thousands of learners enrolled annually
reach_estimate.basis: Language training is among the most-used settlement services

economic_impact.note: Language proficiency directly correlates with employment outcomes and earnings for newcomers

supporting.name: Get information on living and working in Alberta before you arrive
supporting.desc: Prospective and incoming immigrants can access guides, videos, and resources about life in Alberta including housing, health care, education, transportation, and workplace culture through the Opportunity Alberta hub.
supporting.users: Prospective immigrants, approved applicants preparing to move

channels.label: Newcomer's Guide to Working in Alberta

reach_estimate.estimate: Hundreds of thousands of page views and downloads
reach_estimate.basis: Pre-arrival information is heavily used by approved immigrants and prospective applicants

economic_impact.note: Pre-arrival preparation reduces settlement challenges and accelerates integration

main_services.name: Get your foreign credentials recognized in Alberta

supporting.name: Get your international qualifications assessed
supporting.desc: The International Qualifications Assessment Service (IQAS) assesses foreign educational credentials against Alberta standards. An IQAS certificate is often required for immigration applications, professional licensing, and employment.
supporting.users: Internationally educated professionals, immigration applicants, employers

reach_estimate.estimate: Tens of thousands of assessments annually
reach_estimate.basis: IQAS is a primary credential assessment service for Alberta immigration and licensing

economic_impact.note: Credential assessment is the gateway to professional licensing and skilled employment for newcomers

relationships.service: Navigate the licensing process for your regulated profession

supporting.name: Navigate the licensing process for your regulated profession
supporting.desc: Internationally trained professionals in over 80 regulated occupations can access guidance on the licensing process, connect with their Professional Regulatory Organization, and access bridging programs and financial supports to help them complete requirements.
supporting.users: Internationally trained professionals in regulated occupations

reach_estimate.estimate: Thousands of professionals navigating licensing annually
reach_estimate.basis: 80+ regulated professions with internationally trained applicants

economic_impact.note: Fair credential recognition unlocks skilled employment and addresses professional labour shortages

supporting.name: Find employment mentorship and career supports for newcomers
supporting.desc: Newcomers can access career mentorship programs, job search workshops, and employment bridging supports that help them understand the Alberta labour market, build professional networks, and find work matching their skills and experience.
supporting.users: Job-seeking newcomers, internationally trained workers

reach_estimate.estimate: Thousands of newcomers participating annually
reach_estimate.basis: Employment integration is a top priority for new immigrants

economic_impact.note: Mentorship and career support reduce the gap between newcomer skills and Alberta employment

main_services.name: Access multiculturalism, human rights, and inclusion supports

supporting.name: Apply for a multiculturalism or community inclusion grant
supporting.desc: Cultural and community organizations can apply for grants to support events, programs, and initiatives that promote cross-cultural understanding, celebrate diversity, and build inclusive communities across Alberta.
supporting.users: Cultural organizations, community groups, non-profits

reach_estimate.estimate: Hundreds of grant applications annually
reach_estimate.basis: Provincial grant programs supporting cultural community organizations

economic_impact.note: Community grants strengthen social cohesion and support cultural organizations province-wide

supporting.name: File a human rights complaint about discrimination
supporting.desc: Albertans who have experienced discrimination based on race, colour, ancestry, place of origin, religious beliefs, gender, disability, or other protected grounds can file a complaint with the Alberta Human Rights Commission for investigation and resolution.
supporting.users: Individuals who have experienced discrimination in employment, services, or housing

channels.label: Commission offices in Edmonton and Calgary

reach_estimate.estimate: Thousands of complaints and inquiries annually
reach_estimate.basis: Alberta Human Rights Commission receives complaints from across the province

economic_impact.note: Complaint mechanism enforces anti-discrimination protections and deters discriminatory practices

relationships.service: Fund and coordinate settlement, integration, and multiculturalism programs

supporting.name: Learn about human rights protections and anti-racism resources
supporting.desc: Albertans can access educational resources on human rights protections, anti-racism initiatives, and cultural diversity. Resources include guides on protected grounds, employer obligations, and how to promote inclusive workplaces and communities.
supporting.users: All Albertans, employers, educators, community leaders

reach_estimate.estimate: Hundreds of thousands of resource views and downloads
reach_estimate.basis: Broad public interest in human rights education and workplace inclusion

economic_impact.note: Human rights education prevents discrimination and fosters inclusive communities and workplaces

supporting.name: Nominate an immigrant for an Alberta recognition award
supporting.desc: Albertans can nominate outstanding immigrants who have made significant contributions to their communities for recognition through the Alberta Immigrant Impact Awards, celebrating the achievements and contributions of newcomers.
supporting.users: Community members, organizations, employers wishing to recognize immigrant contributions

reach_estimate.estimate: Hundreds of nominations annually
reach_estimate.basis: Annual recognition program for immigrant community contributions

economic_impact.note: Public recognition builds welcoming culture and encourages immigrant civic participation
